title: D² protocol-audiences spec
description: Schema for contracts/auth-protocol-audiences/protocol-audiences.spec.json — the single source of truth for the PROTOCOL audiences (d2.internal, d2-edge). These are NOT token-exchange targets (those live in auth-audiences/audiences.spec.json as URL-shaped entries); they are bare-token protocol constants. The C# WellKnownAudiences.g.cs is emitted by DcsvIo.D2.Auth.ProtocolAudiences.SourceGen; the TS ProtocolAudiences const-object is emitted by the TypeScript codegen pipeline. The @d2Audience validator accepts a value iff it appears here.

name.description: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE identifier — emitted as the const name (e.g. 'D2_INTERNAL_AUDIENCE' → WellKnownAudiences.D2_INTERNAL_AUDIENCE / ProtocolAudiences.D2_INTERNAL_AUDIENCE). Must start with an uppercase letter and contain only uppercase letters, digits, and underscores.

value.description: The bare-token aud-claim value (e.g. 'd2.internal', 'd2-edge'). The const's value is this string. Intentionally NOT URL-shaped — protocol audiences are bare tokens, unlike the URL-shaped token-exchange targets in auth-audiences.

description.description: Free-form description rendered as XML doc on the emitted constant.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
